results.title: Fruit fly
results.creator_credit: Norman A. Uren and Western Australian Government Film Unit
results.brief_description: The Mediterranean fruit fly is a serious pest of the fruit industry in Western Australia. It infests so many different types of fruit that it keeps alive throughout the seasons. It is the larvae that do the damage. It is necessary to keep orchards absolutely clean. One dirty orchard can affect the whole district.

results.title: Beaches and boats
results.creator_credit: Leith Goodall, Norman A. Uren and Western Australian Government Film Unit
results.brief_description: This film depicts the beauty of Western Australia’s beaches and the lifestyle that can be enjoyed there. Sandy beaches stretch along the West Australian coast for hundreds of miles, providing an ideal place for a holiday of boating, water-skiing, surfboard riding, and sunbathing.

results.title: Roundabout
results.creator_credit: Rachel Griffiths, Jason Byrne, Louise Smith, 3rd Pictures and Igloo Films
results.brief_description: A stressed businessman experiences a panic attack after he hits a dog in his car on his way to work. Spiralling out of control he drives in endless circles, unable to stop. Photography, Tristan Milani ; music, David Bridie ; editing, Geordie Anderson. Cast: David Roberts, Alison Whyte, Kym Gyngell.

results.title: Finding the way home
results.creator_credit: Julie McDonald and Children's Welfare Association of Victoria
results.brief_description: Foster parents in rural Victoria discuss what it’s like to care for a foster child, covering issues such as selecting a child, financial implications, emotional support, and most importantly, the benefits. Families interviewed include a single mother with children, an older childless couple, and a middle aged couple with their own children. A young man whose lived with a foster family also shares his experience. Worthwhile viewing for anyone considering fostering a child. Supported by the Federal Department of Family and Community Services.

results.title: The Moonage daydreams of Charlene Stardust
results.creator_credit: Digby Duncan, Jan Carroll, Jan Milne, Margot Oliver and Women's Film Workshop
results.brief_description: Charlene Stardust dreams of her fantasy date in anticipation of her approaching weekend date. As the time, approaches Charlene has become disenchanted by the conventional male and female roles and realises it’s a change, not marriage, that she really needs.

results.title: True faith
results.creator_credit: Lawrence Johnston and Swinburne Institute of Technology. Film and Television School
results.brief_description: True Faith is a dramatic examination of fidelity and relationships in the era of AIDS. At a nightclub, Alan bumps into Stewart, a past lover who has just recently returned to Melbourne. Stewart has been tested as HIV-Positive and his lover has recently died as a result of AIDS. Allan decides to get tested for AIDS. This is a delicately handled drama about a young man coming to terms with an HIV-Positive result, and how to break the news to his partner. Cast includes Dale Knight, Eric Scherer, Colin Batrouney.

results.title: Australia's New Faces 1969 Grand Final
results.creator_credit: Desmond Tester, John Proper, Peter Faiman and GTV9 Melbourne
results.brief_description: Out of 328 acts judged throughout 1969 are the 12 finalists. The winner will receive a $5,000 contract with the Nine Network. The winners - The Paul MacKay Sound; the judges - John Laws, Geoff Brook, Tom Miller, Pat London, Jim McKay. Hosted by Terry Dear and Frank Wilson, voice over by Pete Smith and sponsored by Kevin Dennis. Performing Contestants include Paul Mackay Sound and Mike McClellan.

results.title: Honey harvest
results.creator_credit: Norman A. Uren, Department of Agriculture Western Australia and Western Australian Government Film Unit
results.brief_description: Shows the gathering of honey from wildflowers. It is a seasonal activity and beekeepers move the hives to follow the honey flow throughout the state. The film depicts the extraction of honey, packing and bulk handling. Honey is loaded at Fremantle and shipped overseas. Commercial uses of honey are also indicated.

results.title: Trees for the wheatlands
results.creator_credit: Norman A. Uren and Western Australian Government Film Unit
results.brief_description: The importance of trees for shade and shelter, beautification, and control of wind erosion in certain areas of the wheatbelt. The Forests Department of Western Australia renders valuable service to farmers and others who desire to plant trees on their properties.

results.title: Perth's Mitchell Freeway
results.creator_credit: Norman A. Uren and Western Australian Government Film Unit
results.brief_description: The application of the Mitchell Freeway plan to Perth, its immediate advantage in handling growing traffic densities and the long term implications of city development, are seen in their close inter-relationships.
